active_families.thesis: Channel-switch short bias has tradable density; failure is concentrated in ranging regime, so regime restriction is now the key lever.
Funding-extreme snapback shows real edge with high trade count; objective is to push net edge above cost drag via reward-shape refinement.
TAO pivot-supertrend is regime-fragile, with positive edge in transitional windows; isolate transitional regime before abandoning.
v1 produced zero trades; density-unlock refinement lowers funding threshold to validate whether ETH can support this architecture.
Aggregate edge is negative, but range-specific PF is >1.4 in long-bias branch; one final regime-isolation probe determines retirement.

abandoned_this_cycle.reason: Three-plus iterations with persistent one-trade outcomes; PF is non-inferential and non-scalable.
Three-plus iterations with one-trade pathology despite threshold relaxations.
Repeated anti-edge with PF=0 and <15 trades across variants; entry logic does not map to observed market behavior.
Very low density (2 trades) with PF=0; architecture is too restrictive and low quality.
Structural zero-trade failure after defensive and balanced variants.
Low density (<15 trades), PF 0.28, and no regime where PF exceeds 1.0.
Two variants both deeply negative (PF 0.23, DD 19.6%); transitional pocket is still sub-1 and does not justify further capital.

new_this_cycle.thesis: Rescue attempt by isolating the only profitable regime slice (ranging) seen in long-bias carry-squeeze results.
